steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Central Ideas and Details. The question asks what Jones and Dimaline share. The decisive clues are explicit: Jones is Blackfeet and writes horror; Dimaline is Métis and has “also written in the genre” (horror).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Both have published fictional works featuring main characters who are Blackfeet.": the passage states Jones’s novel features Blackfeet characters, but says nothing about Dimaline’s characters being Blackfeet — her novel is set in a Métis community. Misapplies a detail about Jones to Dimaline. ✗
  • "Both are citizens of the Blackfeet Nation.": Jones is Blackfeet; Dimaline is explicitly identified as Métis. The passage gives different tribal identities for each author. ✗
  • "Both are Indigenous authors who have written horror fiction.": Jones is Blackfeet (Indigenous) and writes horror; Dimaline is Métis (Indigenous) and has “also written in the genre.” Both conditions are directly stated in the text for both authors. ✓
  • "Both have said that they don't like reading horror fiction even though they write it.": the passage never mentions either author’s reading preferences. This is fabricated detail not supported by anything in the text. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
